pullRequest.title: DOM-55601 e2e tests to use a async compliant custom environment instead of DSE

nodes.body: this can be flaky I think since it's trying to install a package for every test run and depends on upstream, we should rather create a docker image from an image of minimal env and use that image to create environment

At the moment I'm reusing already existed steps and practices.
We were doing the same steps for running async models on GPU before, and it was stable.
I will talk to qe-team about it

I think we should do these steps (pip install etc) in some test somewhere if this is what we're expecting our customers to do.
If we're going to provide a pre-existing image to our customers, then we could test that.  But I think we landed on not doing that and just providing instructions/documentation on how to setup an env for async models.

I agree we should do some test but depending every night on upstream might be hard and more expensive to maintain I think, we can have a separate test that runs for release qualification. Let's see what @ddl-bcolby and team thinks
hmm, would we do the release qualification test with every cloud release?
